positioning.oneLiner: The CARE Coaching Needs Assessment (CCNA) turns school practices into action-based data for improvement planning.
positioning.summary: CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and gives education leaders action-based data to support school improvement and professional development planning. Its source language emphasizes instructional practice focus, multilingual learner support alongside broader school-wide practices, actionable reporting, and shared starting points for improving instruction.
corePillars: School improvement and professional development planning

audiences.name: school leaders
needs: Understand actual practices across the school
Use data to identify improvement areas
Plan professional learning with shared, actionable starting points
approvedMessages: CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data.
CCNA focuses on the work through assessment, data analysis, and action planning.
CCNA gives educators shared, actionable starting points for improving instruction and supporting all learners.

audiences.name: district leaders
needs: Compare practices affecting general education and multilingual learner education
Support school teams with prioritized next steps
approvedMessages: CCNA integrates content on multilingual learners alongside general instructional practices, so teams do not need separate tools.
CCNA is task-oriented so school teams can spend more time addressing needs rather than speculating about what needs to improve.
CCNA reporting supports prioritized actions, coaching suggestions, professional development planning, and strategic next steps.
ctaSuggestions: Explore action-based data with CARE Coaching

proofPoints: The CCNA service page says CCNA identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data.
The CCNA service page says the assessment activity focuses on multilingual learner support while also covering broader practices that affect all students.
The CCNA service page contrasts task-oriented data with traditional climate surveys and emphasizes instructional decisions.
The CCNA service page lists action-oriented data, instructional practice focus, actionable reporting, and multilingual learner support as highlights.
approvedPhrases: school improvement and professional development planning
restrictedClaims: Do not guarantee measurable improvement outcomes.
Do not describe CCNA as a traditional climate survey.
Do not claim CCNA evaluates individual educators or proves teacher effectiveness.
Do not imply CCNA is only for multilingual learner programs; sources also describe broader practices affecting all students.

flyer.priority: explain action-based data and drive demo requests

one-pager.priority: compare CCNA to traditional needs assessment and climate surveys

html-email-announcement.priority: invite leaders to request a demo or sample report

sourceEvidence.label: CCNA overview
sourceEvidence.excerpt: The CARE Coaching Needs Assessment (CCNA) identifies strengths, uncovers growth areas, and guides education leaders with action-based data to support school improvement and professional development planning.

sourceEvidence.label: Instructional practice focus
sourceEvidence.excerpt: CCNA focuses on what drives learning: planning, collaboration, feedback, and leadership routines that directly influence teaching.

sourceEvidence.label: Multilingual learner support
sourceEvidence.excerpt: CCNA includes focused items and recommendations on how to better serve multilingual learners, while also providing strategies that benefit all students.

sourceEvidence.label: Actionable reporting
sourceEvidence.excerpt: School teams gain access to user-friendly interactive reports with prioritized actions and coaching suggestions, ready to use for PD planning and strategic next steps.
